subject: Science
question_text: When an electric current flows through a copper wire AB as shown in Figure 14.1, the wire
options: deflects a magnetic needle placed near it.
becomes red hot.
gives electric shock.
behaves like a fuse.
answer_key: deflects a magnetic needle placed near it.
answer_explanation: When an electric current passes through a wire, it behaves like a magnet. This is the magnetic effect of the electric current.

subject: Science
question_text: Choose the statement which is not correct in the case of an electric fuse.
options: Fuses are inserted in electric circuits of all buildings.
There is a maximum limit on the current which can safely flow through the electric circuits.
There is a minimum limit on the current which can safely flow in the electric circuits.
If a proper fuse is inserted in a circuit it will blow off if current exceeds the safe limit.
answer_key: There is a minimum limit on the current which can safely flow in the electric circuits.
answer_explanation: There is no minimum limit to cause a safety concern in standard circuits. Safety devices like fuses respond when current exceeds the *maximum* safe limit.
provenance_excerpt: Choose the statement which is not correct in the case of an electric fuse.

subject: Science
question_text: Three bulbs A, B, C are connected in a circuit as shown in Figure 14.2. When the switch is ‘ON’
options: bulb C will glow first.
bulb B and C will glow simulaneously and bulb A will glow after some time.
all the bulbs A,B and C will glow at the same time.
the bulbs will glow in the order A, B and C.
answer_key: all the bulbs A,B and C will glow at the same time.
answer_explanation: The electric current flows almost instantaneously throughout the closed circuit, causing all bulbs in a series (or parallel) circuit to light up at the same time.

subject: Science
question_text:
When a switch is in OFF position,
(i) circuit starting from the positive terminal of the cell stops at the switch.
(ii) circuit is open.
(iii) no current flows through it.
(iv) current flows after some time.
Choose the combination of correct answer from the following.
options: all are correct
(ii) and (iii) are correct
only (iv) is correct
only (i) and (ii) are correct
answer_key: (ii) and (iii) are correct
answer_explanation: When the switch is OFF, the circuit is broken or 'open', and so no electric current flows through any part of the circuit.

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following precautions need not be taken while using electric gadgets/appliances/circuit?
options: We should never touch a lighted electric bulb connected to the mains.
We should never experiment with the electric supply from the mains or a generator or an inverter.
We should never use just any wire or strip of metal in place of a fuse.
We should never turn the switch in ON position.
answer_key: We should never turn the switch in ON position.
answer_explanation: Turning the switch ON is the basic way to operate an appliance; it's not a safety hazard under normal, safe operating conditions.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following precautions need not be taken while using electric gadgets/appliances/circuit?

subject: Science
question_text: Which property of a conducting wire is utilised in making electric fuse?
answer_key: The property utilised is its low melting point. A fuse wire easily melts and breaks the circuit when excess current flows through it.
provenance_excerpt: Which property of a conducting wire is utilised in making electric fuse?

question_text: Name the device used these days in place of electric fuses in electrical circuits.
answer_key: Miniature Circuit Breakers (MCBs).

subject: Science
question_text: Paheli does not have a night lamp in her room. She covered the bulb of her room with a towel in the night to get dim light. Has she taken the right step? Give one reason to justify your answer.
answer_key: No, she has not taken the right step. A lighted bulb gets very hot due to the heating effect of electric current. Covering it with a towel inhibits heat dissipation and can cause the towel to catch fire.
answer_explanation: Safety regarding the heating effect of current.

subject: Science
question_text: Why are compact fluorescent lamps (CFLs) preferred over electric bulbs?
answer_key: CFLs do not waste electrical energy in the form of heat unlike traditional incandescent bulbs, meaning they are much more energy efficient.
provenance_excerpt: Why are compact fluorescent lamps (CFLs) preferred over electric bulbs?

subject: Science
question_text: Why is an electric fuse required in all electrical appliances?
answer_key: An electric fuse breaks the circuit if the current exceeds a safe limit, preventing damage to the appliance from overheating and averting potential fires.
provenance_excerpt: Why is an electric fuse required in all electrical appliances?

subject: Science
question_text: Can we use the same fuse in a geyser and a television set? Explain.
answer_key: No. A geyser consumes much more power and draws a higher current than a television set. Thus, a geyser requires a fuse with a much higher current-carrying capacity (rating) than a television set.
provenance_excerpt: Can we use the same fuse in a geyser and a television set? Explain.

subject: Science
question_text: Name two electric devices for each where (i) heating effect of current is used and (ii) magnetic effect of current is used.
answer_key:
(i) Heating effect: Electric iron, electric room heater (or geyser).
(ii) Magnetic effect: Electric bell, electromagnet (or electric motor).

subject: Science
question_text: Why do we cover plug pin holes which are within the reach of children with cellotape or a plastic cover when not in use?
answer_key: To prevent children from accidentally inserting their fingers or any metallic objects into the holes, which could result in a severe or fatal electric shock.
provenance_excerpt: Why do we cover plug pin holes which are within the reach of children with cellotape or a plastic cover when not in use?

subject: Science
question_text: Boojho made an electromagnet by winding 50 turns of wire over an iron screw. Paheli also made an electromagnet by winding 100 turns over a similar iron screw. Which electro magnet will attract more pins? Give reason.
answer_key: Paheli's electromagnet will attract more pins. The strength of an electromagnet increases with the number of turns of the coil (assuming identical current and core material).

subject: Science
question_text:
Paheli took a wire of length 10 cm. Boojho took a wire of 5 cm of the same material and thickness. Both of them connected the wires as shown in the circuit given in Figure 14.4. The current flowing in both the circuits is the same.
(i) Will the heat produced in both the cases be equal? Explain.
(ii) Will the heat produced be the same if the wires taken by them are of equal lengths but of different thickness? Explain.
answer_key:
(i) No. The amount of heat produced depends on the length of the wire. A longer wire (10 cm) has more resistance and thus will produce more heat than the shorter wire (5 cm) at the same current.
(ii) No. The amount of heat produced also depends on the thickness of the wire. A thinner wire has more resistance, and so will produce more heat than a thicker wire of the same length and material.
answer_explanation: Dependence of heating effect on wire dimensions.
provenance_excerpt: Paheli took a wire of length 10 cm. Boojho took a wire of 5 cm of the same material and thickness. Both of them connecte

subject: Science
question_text: How does the magnetic effect of electric current help in the working of an electric bell? Explain with the help of a diagram.
answer_key: An electric bell contains an electromagnet. When the switch is pressed, current flows through the coil, making it an electromagnet. It attracts an iron armature, making the attached hammer strike the gong (ringing). This movement also breaks the circuit at the contact screw. The electromagnet loses its magnetism, the armature springs back, the circuit is completed again, and the process repeats continuously.
answer_explanation: Operation mechanism of an electric bell.
